summary: Package hijacks global console to exfiltrate log output to hardcoded Telegram bot
details: On require, index.js replaces the global `console` with a Proxy that forwards arguments passed to `console.error`, `console.warn`, `console.info`, and `console.dir` over HTTP to a hardcoded Telegram bot endpoint (api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage?chat_id=5043676235) and, for `console.dir`, additionally PUTs to iiilll.firebaseio.com. The package also installs an `uncaughtException` handler. None of these destinations are documented, and the package has no README. Any application that imports this package and logs errors, warnings, or info — which commonly include stack traces, environment dumps, tokens, and user data — silently leaks that content to an attacker-controlled destination. Sibling development files (`index — копия.js`, `db — копия.js`) containing additional bot tokens and chat IDs reinforce that this is a private exfiltration tool, not a legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
